title: Obione cuneata
taxonomy: Obione cuneata is classified within the family Amaranthaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family. More specifically, it belongs to the subfamily Salsoloideae within Amaranthaceae. The classification also places it in the order Caryophyllales and the class Magnoliopsida. Further classification details can be found in the taxonomic hierarchy of plant species.

intro: Obione cuneata, also known as the wedge-leaved sea spurrey, is a succulent plant species native to coastal areas of South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat skin conditions such as eczema, wounds, and ulcers, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Obione cuneata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional folk medicine, Obione cuneata is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, which can also be used topically to treat skin conditions, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic properties.
